reference: Low blood pressure can cause lethargy, malaise, severe weakness, fatigue, and nonspecific complaints. If your blood pressure is low, you need to find out the cause of it and need to see a doctor for that. Get your blood checked for hemoglobin, vitamin B12, vitamin D3, calcium, thyroid function, and glucose level. All of this may help to provide some clues regarding your illness.
